words.definitionEn: an occasion when people come together to discuss or decide something
words.exampleEn: A hundred people attended the public meeting.

words.definitionEn: a person, an animal or a plant that belongs to a particular group
words.exampleEn: member of something, a member of the family/community

words.definitionEn: a list of the food that is available at a restaurant or to be served at a meal
words.exampleEn: May we have the menu?

words.definitionEn: used to describe somebody/something appearing to be somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: They were all dressed as clowns.

words.definitionEn: a written or spoken piece of information, etc. that you send to somebody or leave for somebody when you cannot speak to them yourself
words.exampleEn: There were no messages for me at the hotel.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring length; a hundred centimetres
words.exampleEn: a 50-metre swimming pool

words.definitionEn: 12 o’clock at night
words.exampleEn: She heard the clock strike midnight.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring distance equal to 1 609 metres or 1 760 yards
words.exampleEn: a 20-mile drive to work

words.definitionEn: the white liquid produced by cows, goats and some other animals as food for their young and used as a drink by humans
words.exampleEn: a pint/litre of milk
a population of half a million

words.definitionEn: each of the 60 parts of an hour, that are equal to 60 seconds
words.exampleEn: minutes to…, It's four minutes to six.

words.definitionEn: to say or write something in the form of a question, in order to get information
words.exampleEn: How old are you—if you don’t mind me/my asking?

words.definitionEn: to be or arrive too late for something
words.exampleEn: If I don't leave now I'll miss my plane.

words.definitionEn: an action or an opinion that is not correct, or that produces a result that you did not want
words.exampleEn: It's easy to make a mistake.

words.definitionEn: a copy of something, usually smaller than the original object
words.exampleEn: a working model (= one in which the parts move) of a fire engine

words.definitionEn: of the present time or recent times
words.exampleEn: the modern industrial world

words.definitionEn: a very short period of time
words.exampleEn: Could you wait a moment, please?

words.definitionEn: the day of the week after Sunday and before Tuesday, the first day of the working week
words.exampleEn: It's Monday today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: what you earn by working or selling things, and use to buy things
words.exampleEn: to borrow/save/spend/earn money

words.definitionEn: any of the twelve periods of time into which the year is divided, for example May or June
words.exampleEn: The months of July and August are the hottest.

words.definitionEn: used to form the comparative of most adjectives and adverbs with two or more syllables
words.exampleEn: She was far more intelligent than her sister.

words.definitionEn: a larger number or amount of
words.exampleEn: I want some more!

words.definitionEn: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: We don't have very many copies left.

words.definitionEn: the early part of the day from the time when people wake up until 12 o'clock in the middle of the day or before lunch
words.exampleEn: They left for Spain early this morning.

words.definitionEn: used to form the superlative of most adjectives and adverbs of two or more syllables
words.exampleEn: the most boring/beautiful part

words.definitionEn: the largest in number or amount
words.exampleEn: Who do you think will get (the) most votes?

words.definitionEn: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: We don't have very many copies left.

words.definitionEn: a female parent of a child or animal; a person who is acting as a mother to a child
words.exampleEn: I want to buy a present for my mother and father.

words.definitionEn: a very high hill, often with rocks near the top
words.exampleEn: the mountains of Andalusia

words.definitionEn: a small animal that is covered in fur and has a long thin tail. Mice live in fields, in people’s houses or where food is stored.
words.exampleEn: a house mouse

words.definitionEn: the opening in the face used for speaking, eating, etc.; the area inside the head behind this opening
words.exampleEn: She opened her mouth to say something.

words.definitionEn: to change position or make somebody/something change position in a way that can be seen, heard or felt
words.exampleEn: Don't move—stay perfectly still.

words.definitionEn: a series of moving pictures recorded with sound that tells a story, watched at a movie theater or on a television or other device
words.exampleEn: You'll love this movie.

words.definitionEn: to a great degree
words.exampleEn: Thank you very much for the flowers.

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns, especially in negative sentences to mean ‘a large amount of something’, or after ‘how’ to ask about the amount of something. It is also used with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: I don't have much money with me.

words.definitionEn: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: We don't have very many copies left.

words.definitionEn: a mother
words.exampleEn: Call your mum.

words.definitionEn: a building in which objects of artistic, cultural, historical or scientific interest are kept and shown to the public
words.exampleEn: a museum of modern art

words.definitionEn: sounds that are arranged in a way that is pleasant or exciting to listen to. People sing music or play it on instruments.
words.exampleEn: I like any kind of pop or dance music.

words.definitionEn: used to say that something is necessary or very important (sometimes involving a rule or a law)
words.exampleEn: All visitors must report to reception.

words.definitionEn: of or belonging to the speaker or writer
words.exampleEn: Where's my passport?

words.definitionEn: a word or words that a particular person, animal, place or thing is known by
words.exampleEn: What's your name?

words.definitionEn: to give a name to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: name somebody/something (after somebody), He was named after his father (= given his father's first name).

words.definitionEn: existing in nature; not made or caused by humans
words.exampleEn: the natural world (= of trees, rivers, animals and birds)

words.definitionEn: a short distance away
words.exampleEn: His house is very near.

words.definitionEn: at a short distance away
words.exampleEn: A bomb exploded somewhere near.

words.definitionEn: at a short distance away from somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Do you live near here?

words.definitionEn: to require something/somebody because they are essential or very important, not just because you would like to have them
words.exampleEn: need something/somebody, Do you need any help?

words.definitionEn: bad or harmful
words.exampleEn: The crisis had a negative effect on trade.

words.definitionEn: a person who lives next to you or near you
words.exampleEn: We've had a lot of support from all our friends and neighbours.

words.definitionEn: not at any time; not on any occasion
words.exampleEn: You never help me.

words.definitionEn: not existing before; recently made, invented, introduced, etc.
words.exampleEn: Have you read her new novel?

words.definitionEn: new information about something that has happened recently
words.exampleEn: What's the latest news?

words.definitionEn: a set of large printed sheets of paper, or a website, containing news, articles, advertisements, etc. and published every day or every week; the organization responsible for producing this
words.exampleEn: a daily/weekly newspaper

words.definitionEn: coming straight after somebody/something in time, order or space
words.exampleEn: The next train to Baltimore is at ten.

words.definitionEn: after something else; then; afterwards
words.exampleEn: What happened next?

words.definitionEn: in or into a position right by somebody/something
words.exampleEn: We sat next to each other.

words.definitionEn: pleasant or attractive
words.exampleEn: a nice day/smile/place

words.definitionEn: the time between one day and the next when it is dark, when people usually sleep
words.exampleEn: at night, These animals only come out at night.
There are only nine of these rare animals left.
The temperature must be in the nineties today.

words.definitionEn: not one; not any; not a
words.exampleEn: No student is to leave the room.

words.definitionEn: used to give a negative reply or statement
words.exampleEn: Just say yes or no.

words.definitionEn: not anyone; no person
words.exampleEn: No one was at home.

words.definitionEn: not anyone; no person
words.exampleEn: Nobody knew what to say.

words.definitionEn: in or towards the north
words.exampleEn: North London

words.definitionEn: towards the north
words.exampleEn: The house faces north.

words.definitionEn: the direction that is on your left when you watch the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass
words.exampleEn: Which way is north?

words.definitionEn: the part of the face that sticks out above the mouth, used for breathing and smelling things
words.exampleEn: She had dark eyes and a long narrow nose.

words.definitionEn: used with be, do or have to form the negative of verbs; used to form the negative of modal verbs like can or must
words.exampleEn: She did not/didn’t see him.

words.definitionEn: a short piece of writing to help you remember something
words.exampleEn: Please make a note of the dates.

words.definitionEn: not anything; no single thing
words.exampleEn: There was nothing in her bag.

words.definitionEn: the 11th month of the year, between October and December
words.exampleEn: She was born in November.

words.definitionEn: (at) the present time
words.exampleEn: Where are you living now?

words.definitionEn: a word or symbol that represents an amount or a quantity
words.exampleEn: Think of a number and multiply it by two.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to take care of sick or injured people, usually in a hospital
words.exampleEn: a registered nurse

words.definitionEn: used with the numbers 1 to 12 when telling the time, to mean an exact hour
words.exampleEn: He left between five and six o'clock.

words.definitionEn: a thing that can be seen and touched, but is not alive
words.exampleEn: everyday objects such as cups and saucers

words.definitionEn: the 10th month of the year, between September and November
words.exampleEn: She was born in October.

words.definitionEn: belonging to somebody; relating to somebody
words.exampleEn: a friend of mine

words.definitionEn: used to say that something has been removed or become separated
words.exampleEn: He's had his beard shaved off.

words.definitionEn: down or away from a place or at a distance in space or time
words.exampleEn: I fell off the ladder.

words.definitionEn: a room, set of rooms or building where people work, usually sitting at desks
words.exampleEn: The company is moving to new offices on the other side of town.

words.definitionEn: many times
words.exampleEn: We often go there.

words.definitionEn: used when you are reacting to something that has been said, especially if you did not know it before
words.exampleEn: ‘I saw Ben yesterday.’ ‘Oh yes, how is he?’

words.definitionEn: safe and well; in a calm or happy state
words.exampleEn: Are you OK?

words.definitionEn: safe and well; in a calm or happy state
words.exampleEn: Are you OK?

words.definitionEn: yes; all right
words.exampleEn: ‘Shall we go for a walk?’ ‘OK.’

words.definitionEn: of a particular age
words.exampleEn: be… years, months, etc. old, The baby was only a few hours old.

words.definitionEn: on somebody’s body; being worn
words.exampleEn: Put your coat on.

words.definitionEn: in or into a position covering, touching or forming part of a surface
words.exampleEn: a picture on a wall

words.definitionEn: on one occasion only; one time
words.exampleEn: I've only been there once.

words.definitionEn: the number 1
words.exampleEn: Do you want one or two?

words.definitionEn: the number 1
words.exampleEn: Do you want one or two?

words.definitionEn: used to avoid repeating a noun, when you are referring to somebody/something that has already been mentioned, or that the person you are speaking to knows about
words.exampleEn: I'd like an ice cream. Are you having one, too?

words.definitionEn: a round vegetable with many layers inside each other and a brown, red or white skin. Onions have a strong smell and taste.
words.exampleEn: Chop the onions finely.

words.definitionEn: available on or done using the internet or other computer network
words.exampleEn: Online shopping is both cheap and convenient.

words.definitionEn: onto the internet; using the internet or other computer network
words.exampleEn: Many children would rather go online than watch television.

words.definitionEn: used to say that no other or others of the same group exist or are there
words.exampleEn: She's their only daughter.

words.definitionEn: nobody or nothing except
words.exampleEn: There are only a limited number of tickets available.

words.definitionEn: allowing things or people to go through; not closed or blocked
words.exampleEn: A wasp flew in the open window.

words.definitionEn: to move a door, window, lid, etc. into a position that is no longer closed; to get into this position
words.exampleEn: Mr Chen opened the car door for his wife.

words.definitionEn: used to say where something/somebody is or where something happens
words.exampleEn: at the corner of the street

words.definitionEn: your feelings or thoughts about somebody/something, rather than a fact
words.exampleEn: He has very strong political opinions.

words.definitionEn: on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something and usually facing them
words.exampleEn: Answers are given on the opposite page.

words.definitionEn: on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something and usually facing them
words.exampleEn: There's a newly married couple living opposite (= on the other side of the road).

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that is as different as possible from somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: Hot and cold are opposites.

words.definitionEn: on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something, and usually facing them
words.exampleEn: I sat opposite him during the meal (= on the other side of the table).

words.definitionEn: used to introduce another possibility
words.exampleEn: Is your sister older or younger than you?

words.definitionEn: between red and yellow in colour
words.exampleEn: yellow and orange flames

words.definitionEn: a round citrus fruit with thick skin of a colour between red and yellow and a lot of sweet juice
words.exampleEn: orange peel/zest/rind

words.definitionEn: the way in which people or things are placed or arranged in relation to each other
words.exampleEn: in… order, The names are listed in alphabetical order.

words.definitionEn: to ask for something to eat or drink in a restaurant, bar, etc.
words.exampleEn: order (something), I ordered a coffee and a sandwich.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to people or things that are additional or different to people or things that have been mentioned or are known about
words.exampleEn: Mr Harris and Mrs Bate and three other teachers were there.

words.definitionEn: used to refer to people or things that are additional or different to people or things that have been mentioned or are known about
words.exampleEn: Mr Harris and Mrs Bate and three other teachers were there.

words.definitionEn: belonging to us; connected with us
words.exampleEn: our daughter/dog/house

words.definitionEn: away from the inside of a place or thing
words.exampleEn: She ran out into the corridor.

words.definitionEn: away from the inside of a place or thing
words.exampleEn: She ran out into the corridor.

words.definitionEn: not in a room, building or container but on or to the outside of it
words.exampleEn: I'm seeing a patient—please wait outside.

words.definitionEn: across a street, an open space, etc.
words.exampleEn: I stopped and crossed over.

words.definitionEn: resting on the surface of somebody/something and partly or completely covering them/it
words.exampleEn: She put a blanket over the sleeping child.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that something belongs to or is connected with somebody
words.exampleEn: It was her own idea.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that something belongs to or is connected with somebody
words.exampleEn: It was her own idea.

words.definitionEn: one side or both sides of a sheet of paper in a book, magazine, etc.
words.exampleEn: Turn to page 64.

words.definitionEn: a liquid that is put on surfaces to give them a particular colour; a layer of this liquid when it has dried on a surface
words.exampleEn: white paint

words.definitionEn: to cover a surface or object with paint
words.exampleEn: paint something with something, Paint the shed with weather-resistant paint.

words.definitionEn: a picture that has been painted
words.exampleEn: a watercolour/crylic painting

words.definitionEn: two things of the same type, especially when they are used or worn together
words.exampleEn: a pair of shoes/boots

words.definitionEn: the thin material that you write and draw on and that is also used for wrapping and packing things
words.exampleEn: a piece/sheet of paper

words.definitionEn: a section of a piece of writing, usually consisting of several sentences dealing with a single subject. The first sentence of a paragraph starts on a new line.
words.exampleEn: an opening/introductory paragraph

words.definitionEn: a person’s father or mother
words.exampleEn: He's still living with his parents.

words.definitionEn: an area of public land in a town or a city where people go to walk, play and relax
words.exampleEn: Hyde Park

words.definitionEn: to leave a vehicle that you are driving in a particular place for a period of time
words.exampleEn: He parked and went inside.

words.definitionEn: some but not all of a thing
words.exampleEn: We spent part of the time in the museum.
